name: CapTable.io
tagline: The cap table every founder actually understands
description: CapTable.io replaces messy spreadsheets and $10K/yr Carta plans with a clean, affordable cap table and equity management platform built for early-stage founders. Real-time modeling, 409A valuations, SAFEs and convertible notes handled automatically.
founders: Ex-Stripe eng + ex-Andreessen Horowitz VC, both founders left Series A-backed startups to build this
moat: Network effects as investors share and refer — 40% of signups come from existing user referrals. Switching cost is extremely high once a cap table is live.
risks: Carta has deep pockets, complex compliance requirements as you scale

name: DealRoom AI
tagline: AI due diligence that reads pitch decks like a seasoned VC
description: DealRoom AI ingests pitch decks, LinkedIn profiles, and public data to automatically generate a structured due diligence memo — market sizing, team analysis, competitive landscape, red flags, and a recommended investment score. Built for angels and micro-VCs drowning in deal flow.
founders: Former product lead at AngelList + MIT ML PhD
moat: Proprietary training data from 50K+ pitch decks. AI that improves with every deal reviewed. Platform lock-in as angels build deal history.
risks: Pre-revenue, unproven AI accuracy, large incumbents could add this feature

name: StockX Pro
tagline: Institutional-grade analytics for serious sneaker resellers
description: StockX Pro gives professional sneaker resellers the data tools that institutions have — real-time price feeds, sell-through rates, demand forecasting, and automated buying alerts. Think Bloomberg Terminal for the $6B sneaker resale market.
founders: Ex-StockX data engineer + former Nike wholesale buyer, domain experts through and through
moat: Exclusive data partnerships with 3 major resale platforms. Proprietary demand signal model trained on 8 years of transaction data.
risks: StockX or GOAT could build competing tools, dependent on API access

name: Relay
tagline: Async video updates that replace your status meetings
description: Relay replaces daily standups, weekly check-ins, and status emails with structured async video updates. Teams record 90-second context videos; Relay's AI summarizes, extracts blockers, and routes action items — cutting meeting time by 40% for distributed teams.
founders: Ex-Google PM (led Meet growth) + ex-Notion design lead, repeat collaborators
moat: Deep Slack/Notion/Jira integrations create switching cost. AI summaries improve per-team over time — better the longer you use it.
risks: Loom is a direct competitor, Zoom adding async features, crowded space

name: Farmr
description: Farmr connects city residents with underutilized backyard land to grow their own produce. Landowners list unused space; renters pay monthly to access micro-plots and farm supplies. Think Airbnb for garden plots.
founders: Two first-time founders, backgrounds in urban planning
moat: Local network effects within neighborhoods. First-mover in 3 target cities.
risks: Very local and niche, cold-start problem in every new city, low willingness to pay
